# Figure description: Картограмма_литогеохимии.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Картограмма_литогеохимии.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index map (обзорная карта / карта листов)** — specifically a **sheet layout / coverage diagram** showing the spatial arrangement of survey map sheets at multiple scales. It is labeled "Литохимия" (Lithogeochemistry) and serves as a catalogue index of lithogeochemical survey coverage, Drawing No. 23, Sheet 5, dated 1984.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers the survey area of the **Западно-Тенизская партия ЦГПЭ (West-Teniz Party, Central Geophysical Prospecting Expedition)** for the period **1978–1984**, as stated in the caption at lower right. The map shows the layout of numbered lithogeochemical survey contours/polygons (контуры по каталогу) organized across the survey area. Roman numerals label major map sheet blocks (e.g., **I, III, IV, V, VII, VIII, IX, XIV, XV, XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV**), corresponding to subdivisions of the regional survey grid, likely within map sheets of the **M-42 and N-42** series referenced in the report context.

---

## Key Content

The figure depicts a **patchwork of rectangular and irregular polygons** representing lithogeochemical survey coverage at **three different scales**, distinguished by hatching style per the legend:

- **Diagonal hatching (one direction):** Scale **1:500,000**
- **Cross-hatching or single dashes:** Scale **1:100,000**
- **Single diagonal lines (opposite direction):** Scale **1:50,000**

Each polygon is labeled with a **catalogue contour number** (номер контура по каталогу), shown in small boxed numerals. Visible contour numbers include: **169, 189, 278, 230, 228, 225, 181, 107, 261, 191, 22.5, 924, 365, 302, 228, 463, 453, 510, 453, 610, 627, 322, 322, 77, 402, 371, 322, 291, 374, 371, 402, 435, 275, 89, 69, 360, 388, 360, 77** (some numbers may be approximate due to image resolution).

The polygons are arranged in a complex, non-contiguous mosaic pattern — characteristic of phased regional lithogeochemical surveys where different areas were covered at different scales and during different field seasons. The overall shape of the combined coverage is irregular, with the densest coverage in the central and upper portions of the sheet, and more dispersed blocks toward the lower-left (sector XIV–XV).

A **stamp/seal** in the lower-right quadrant reads (partially legible): **"...ССР ... [Каз]ахский геологический фонд ... Инв. № 36572"** — confirming this belongs to Report 36572 in the Kazakh Geological Fund.

---

## Text and Labels

**Title (top center):** ЛИТОХИМИЯ

**Top left:** 1984 г.

**Top right:** Чертеж № 23, лист 5

**Bottom right caption:** К отчету Западно-Тенизской партии ЦГПЭ за 1978–84 г.г.

**Legend header:** УСЛОВНЫЕ ОБОЗНАЧЕНИЯ:

**Legend entries:**
- *(diagonal hatch, one direction)* — масштаб 1:500 000
- *(dash pattern)* — — н — — 1:100 000
- *(diagonal hatch, opposite direction)* — масштаб 1:50 000
- *(box with number)* — номер контура по каталогу *(example: 2.91)*

**Roman numeral sheet labels visible:** I, III, IV, V, VII, VIII, IX, XIV, XV, XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV

**Stamp text (partial):** ...ССР / [Каз]ахский / геологический фонд / Инв. № 36572 / [...]72

---

## Research Value

This index map provides a **complete spatial inventory of lithogeochemical sampling coverage** across the West-Teniz survey area, showing which sub-areas were sampled at reconnaissance (1:500,000), semi-detailed (1:100,000), or detailed (1:50,000) scales between 1978 and 1984. For a geologist or AI research assistant, it answers the fundamental question: **"Where was lithogeochemical data collected, at what resolution, and how does coverage correspond to the numbered contour catalogue?"** This is essential for understanding the spatial distribution of geochemical anomalies reported elsewhere in Report 36572, and for identifying areas where detailed follow-up sampling may have targeted prospective zones for mineral exploration.
